source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A rose bush has 8 flowers on which butterflies and dragonflies are sitting. On every flower there is at most one insect sitting on it. More than half of the flowers are occupied. The number of butterflies is twice as big as the number of dragonflies. How many butterflies are sitting on the rose blossoms?
<image1>

image_dependency: question_text is fully self-contained and does not reference a diagram, chart, or visual attribute
image_manifest contains 1 image, but no textual evidence shows the image is necessary to solve the problem
multi_step: The problem combines multiple constraints: 8 flowers, at most one insect per flower, more than half occupied, and butterflies = 2 × dragonflies
Solving requires translating the text into count relations and checking which total insect count satisfies both the occupancy and ratio constraints
This is more than direct retrieval or a single arithmetic operation
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value: 4
The stated conditions can be checked explicitly against the answer using simple arithmetic
registry_passed=true, with question and answer both present in the registry

decision_reasons: question, answer, and image assets are all present, and the registry passed with no recorded issues
the question appears text-dominant and self-contained, so the minor image-dependency flag does not block recoverability or usability
high verifiability score and numeric answer support reliable checking
multi-step score is reasonably strong, indicating useful problem-solving value
